question: Write a C++ program to create a 3x4 integer matrix (2D array). Initialize it with some values, then calculate and print the sum of elements for each individual row.

test_cases.expected:
Sum of Row 0: (sum of 10,20,30,40 + garbage)
Sum of Row 1: (sum of 15,25,35,45 + garbage)
Sum of Row 2: (sum of 5,10,15,20 + garbage)

errors: The inner loop iterates one element too far (j <= COLS instead of j < COLS), leading to an out-of-bounds array access (matrix[i][4]). This is undefined behavior and a robustness issue, as it could lead to crashes or incorrect results depending on the system/compiler.
